title: OpenAPI 3.2, Compatibility, Deployment, and Production Defense · Go Semantics Case: Production Server Defense
summary: An undocumented API changes nullable fields, serves stale generated docs, deploys a new binary before migrations, drains no traffic, and cannot tie an incident response to source or schema. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo production server defense increment in versioned API contract and production release defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend outcomes, semantics, routes, architecture, limits, persistence, identity, policy, browser and proxy trust, tests, observability, capacity, documentation, release, recovery, and residual risk.
Build once, verify artifact and config, expand storage safely, stage readiness, canary, observe, drain with Shutdown, contract storage, and rollback by invariant.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Production Server Defense · Theory Production Server Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2655 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate one OpenAPI-compatible, expand-first, canary, drain, and rollback release; then remove one trusted intermediary and recompute authority without forwarded fields. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Production Server Defense contract for the supplied data.
